system_role: You are an academic assistant trained on the ASTU CSE syllabus. You provide precise, syllabus-aligned explanations, focusing on conceptual clarity, derivations, problem-solving approaches, and exam relevance.

Bridge_Courses.focus: Essential mathematical and programmatic foundations for transition into degree-level engineering.

subjects.name: Advanced Engineering Calculus & Matrices
subjects.context: Fills the gap between diploma-level math and the rigorous Mathematics III requirements.

subjects.name: C Programming Deep Dive
subjects.context: Essential prerequisite for DSA and Object-Oriented Programming.

Semester_3.focus: Introduction to core computing, programming paradigms, and digital logic fundamentals

subjects.name: Mathematics III
subjects.textbook: Higher Engineering Mathematics by B.S. Grewal

subjects.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
subjects.context: Arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, sorting and searching algorithms with time and space complexity analysis.
subjects.textbook: Fundamentals of Data Structures in C++ by Horowitz & Sahni

subjects.name: Digital Systems
subjects.context: Boolean algebra, logic gates, combinational and sequential circuits, minimization techniques, and digital circuit design.
subjects.textbook: Digital Design by M. Morris Mano

subjects.name: Object Oriented Programming (C++)
subjects.textbook: Object Oriented Programming with C++ by E. Balagurusamy

Semester_4.focus: Understanding system-level design and interaction between hardware and software

subjects.name: Operating Systems
subjects.context: Process management, CPU scheduling, deadlocks, memory management, file systems, and synchronization mechanisms.
subjects.textbook: Operating System Concepts by Silberschatz & Galvin

subjects.name: Computer Organization & Architecture
subjects.context: Instruction sets, CPU organization, memory hierarchy, I/O systems, and performance evaluation.
subjects.textbook: Computer System Architecture by M. Morris Mano

Semester_5.focus: Development of theoretical foundations and core hardware/software components

subjects.name: Microprocessor & Microcontroller
subjects.context: Architecture, instruction sets, assembly programming, interfacing, and embedded system basics.

subjects.name: Database Management Systems
subjects.context: Relational model, SQL, normalization, indexing, transactions, concurrency control, and recovery.
subjects.textbook: Database System Concepts by Korth & Silberschatz

subjects.name: Formal Language & Automata Theory
subjects.context: Regular expressions, DFA/NFA, context-free grammars, pushdown automata, and Turing machines.
subjects.textbook: Introduction to Automata Theory by Hopcroft & Ullman

Semester_6.focus: Advanced computing systems, network protocols, and software translation

subjects.name: Compiler Design
subjects.context: Lexical analysis, syntax parsing, semantic analysis, intermediate code generation, optimization, and code generation.
subjects.textbook: Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools by Aho & Ullman

subjects.name: Computer Networks
subjects.context: OSI and TCP/IP models, routing algorithms, congestion control, data link protocols, and network security basics.
subjects.textbook: Data Communications and Networking by Behrouz A. Forouzan

Semester_7.focus: Security, advanced architecture, and real-world system implementation

subjects.name: Cryptography & Information Security
subjects.context: Symmetric/asymmetric encryption, hashing, digital signatures, authentication, and network security protocols.
subjects.textbook: Cryptography and Network Security by William Stallings

subjects.name: Project Phase I
subjects.context: Initial system design, requirement gathering, and architectural planning for a capstone implementation.

Semester_8.focus: Final system delivery and specialized open electives

subjects.name: Major Project
subjects.context: Capstone project involving design, development, and evaluation of a complete system.
